questions.questionText: Which sense organ do we use for hearing?
questions.explanation: We use our ears to hear sounds. Ears are the sense organs for hearing.
hints: Think about which body part helps you hear music

questions.explanation: Every person looks different - different faces, hair, eyes, skin color. This makes each person unique!
hints: Look at your classmates - do they all look the same?

questions.questionText: True or False: Twins always look exactly the same.
questions.explanation: False! Even identical twins have some differences. And many twins are not identical at all - they can look quite different.
hints: Think about twins you might know

questions.questionText: What helps us recognize our friends and family?
questions.explanation: We recognize people by their face features - shape of eyes, nose, smile, face shape, etc.
hints: What do you look at when you see someone?

questions.questionText: Some people cannot hear. They are called:
questions.explanation: People who cannot hear are called deaf. They often use sign language to communicate.

questions.questionText: How do deaf people communicate with others?
questions.explanation: Deaf people use sign language - hand movements and gestures - to communicate. Some also read lips.
hints: Think about using hands to talk

questions.questionText: Which features can be different in people?
questions.explanation: People differ in many features - hair color and type, eye color, nose shape, skin color, height, and more.
hints: Think about all the ways people can look different

questions.questionText: True or False: It's okay that people look different from each other.
questions.explanation: True! Differences make us unique and special. We should respect and celebrate diversity.
hints: Think about how boring it would be if everyone looked the same

questions.questionText: Why do children often look similar to their parents?
options: They get features from their parents
They go to the same school
questions.explanation: Children inherit features from their parents - like eye color, hair type, face shape. This is why families often look similar.
hints: Think about what you got from your mom or dad

questions.questionText: A hearing aid helps people who:
questions.explanation: A hearing aid is a small device worn in or behind the ear that helps people who have difficulty hearing.
hints: The name tells you what it helps with
